channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Severe Water Shortages Threaten Corpus Christi and the Colorado River States
item.description: This summer, the United States is facing serious water crises in both local and regional contexts. Corpus Christi, Texas, is approaching a Level 1 drought emergency due to water demand exceeding suppl...

item.title: KZN Boxing Federation Seeks Support for Community Mega-Gym to Develop Young Fighters
item.description: Sandile Vilakazi, chairperson of the KwaZulu-Natal Boxing Federation, has called on Sports, Arts and Culture Minister Gayton McKenzie to assist in establishing a major boxing facility in Durban. Vilak...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh ULBs report significant increase in property tax collections in April
item.description: In April 2026, Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in Andhra Pradesh collected over ₹1,010.7 crore in property taxes, including arrears and penalties, representing a 24.1 per cent increase compared to ₹814.1 cr...

item.title: Counting of votes for Koridang Assembly Bypoll in Nagaland scheduled for tomorrow
item.description: The counting of votes for the Koridang Assembly bypoll in Nagaland is scheduled to take place tomorrow. This by-election is significant as it will determine the representative for the Koridang constit...

item.title: Zanzibar to expand Fumba road to six lanes ahead of AFCON City development
item.description: Zanzibar is making serious moves to improve its infrastructure, especially around Fumba where the new AFCON City is under construction. The government, through the Presidential Delivery Bureau (PDB), ...

item.title: Austrian Authorities Detain Suspect After Rat Poison Found in Baby Food Jars
item.description: Austrian police have detained a suspect in connection with a concerning incident where rat poison was discovered in jars of baby food on supermarket shelves. Authorities launched a swift investigation...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Results for May 3, 2026 Released
item.description: Inilabas ng ABS-CBN News ang lotto results para sa May 3, 2026, batay sa opisyal na anunsyo ng Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). Ang naturang ulat ay bahagi ng regular na pagbibigay ng imp...

item.title: Maya Jama and Celebrities Arrive in Scotland for Filming of BBC's Celebrity Traitors Series Two
item.description: Maya Jama was spotted at Inverness Airport on Sunday as she arrived to film the second series of BBC's Celebrity Traitors, marking a significant change from her usual sunny filming locations in Spain ...

item.title: Federal Budget to Focus on Savings to Address Inflation and Economic Pressures
item.description: The Albanese government's upcoming budget will prioritise saving rather than spending, in response to the inflationary pressures exacerbated by the ongoing war with Iran. The high commodity prices, dr...

item.title: Banker joins Sexbomb Jopay for a dance segment on Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal
item.description: In a light and entertaining segment of Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal, viewers were treated to a fun surprise as the mysterious “Banker” joined Sexbomb dancer Jopay in a lively dance number. The moment add...

item.title: Alex Ferguson taken to hospital for precautionary checks before Manchester United vs Liverpool clash
item.description: Legendary former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson was taken to hospital on Sunday shortly before the club’s highly anticipated Premier League clash against Liverpool at Old Trafford. Fergus...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword Challenges for May 4
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald presents its Superquiz for Monday, May 4, 2026, offering readers a chance to test their general knowledge and problem-solving skills. Alongside the Superquiz, a Mini Crosswor...

item.title: Mini Crossword Puzzle for Monday, May 4
item.description: The Age has published its Superquiz for Monday, May 4, 2026, inviting readers to try a bite-sized Mini Crossword. The puzzle consists of ten clues across twenty-five squares, offering a quick challeng...

item.title: Israeli Court Extends Detention of Two Gaza-Bound Flotilla Activists for Further Interrogation
item.description: An Israeli court in Ashkelon has extended by two days the detention of two pro-Palestinian activists, Saif Abu Keshek from Spain and Thiago Avila from Brazil, who were part of the Global Sumud Flotill...

item.title: ADB Launches $70bn Plan to Improve Energy and Digital Infrastructure Across Asia-Pacific by 2035
item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has declared a massive $70 billion initiative aimed at improving energy and digital infrastructure across the Asia-Pacific region by 2035. This plan is divided into tw...

item.title: Significant wildlife loss in Moray due to river pollution
item.description: A serious pollution incident in Moray has resulted in the deaths of a large number of fish, eels, and birds in the Knockando burn, which flows into the River Spey. The contaminant is believed to be ca...

item.title: Jarome Luai offers limited comment after difficult week and Tigers loss
item.description: Jarome Luai kept his comments short and guarded following a challenging week in the spotlight, culminating in a heavy loss for the Wests Tigers. The article indicates that Luai, who has been at the ce...

item.title: Well-known Eastern Cape crime fighter killed in Duncan Village shooting
item.description: On Saturday afternoon, Zola “Ta Zet” Titi, a respected crime fighter aged 59, was fatally shot at his tuck shop in Duncan Village, KuGompo City, while with his partner. According to police, a male sus...

item.title: Two women perish in Channel crossing attempt from France
item.description: Two women, approximately 20 years old and believed to be of Sundanese origin, tragically died while attempting to cross the English Channel from France in a small boat. The vessel, carrying 82 people,...

item.title: Jamie’s Italian reopens in central London with smaller restaurant and simplified menu
item.description: Seven years after the collapse of Jamie’s Italian, which left the company £83 million in debt and caused 1,000 job losses, Jamie Oliver has reopened a flagship site in Leicester Square, London. The ne...
